Title: Overexpression of petunia chalcone isomerase in tomato results in fruit containing increased levels of flavonols

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor for flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate-limiting or key control step in tomato fruit peel flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity; potential cardiovascular health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive overexpression increases tomato fruit peel flavonols up to 78-fold, mainly rutin/quercetin glycosides, with reduced naringenin chalcone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tomato fruit peel accumulates naringenin chalcone while endogenous chi transcript is below detection, suggesting limitation at the CHI step. 2. Overexpression of Petunia hybrida chi in tomato strongly increased quercetin and kaempferol glycosides, especially rutin, and depleted naringenin chalcone. 3. The high-flavonol phenotype co-segregated with the chi transgene and remained stable over at least four generations and under field conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; Southern blot copy-number confirmation; Northern blot transcript detection; HPLC metabolite profiling of hydrolyzed and nonhydrolyzed extracts; segregation analysis across generations; field trial evaluation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In study species tomato, transgene-derived chi transcript detected at low levels in peel at all tested fruit developmental stages in high-flavonol transformants; higher transcript levels in leaves and green/breaker/turning flesh, but flavonol increase occurred mainly in peel
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse growth; open field trial evaluation; fruit developmental stage strongly affects flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel; processed tomato paste retains part of peel-derived flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful metabolic engineering target to produce high-flavonol tomato fruit and processed tomato products with enhanced antioxidant-related nutritional value, with stable inheritance and no major visible growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slight dullness in ripe fruit red color tone due to reduced yellow naringenin chalcone in peel; no major vegetative phenotype change; lycopene concentration unchanged
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The experimentally manipulated core gene is the Petunia chi transgene expressed in tomato; endogenous tomato chi is discussed mainly as background/limiting activity. Increased isoquercitrin in strong lines suggests downstream rhamnosyltransferase capacity may become limiting for rutin formation.
